title: Pichai Paathiram Lyrics Naan Kadavul
stanzas:
pindam ennum,
elumbodu sadhai narambu
udhiramum adangiya
udambu enum

pindam ennum,
elumbodu sadhai narambu
udhiramum adangiya
udambu enum

ammaiyum appanum thanthatha
illai aadhiyin val vinai soozhnthatha (2)

immaiyai naan ariyathatha
immaiyai naan ariyathatha
siru bommaiyin nilayinil
unmaiyai unarnthida

athanai selvamum un idathil
naan pichaikku selvathu evvidathil (2)
verum paathiram ullathu en idathil
athan soothiramo athu un idathil

oru muraiya iru muraiya
pala murai pala pirappu
edukka vaithaai

puthu vinaya pazha vinaya,
kanam kanam dinam
 enai thudikka vaithai

un arul arul arul endru
alaikindra manam
indru pithatruthe

arul vizhiyaal nokkuvai
malar pathathal thaanguvai
un thiru karam enai
aravanaithu unatharul pera

pindam ennum,
elumbodu sadhai narambu
udhiramum adangiya

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Pichai Paathiram, the lyrics reflect a deep sense of yearning and devotion, encapsulating the struggles and aspirations of the protagonist in Naan Kadavul. The song conveys a poignant message about the human condition, evoking feelings of melancholy and hope. It serves as a soulful reminder of the spiritual journey one undertakes in life. The soothing melody, combined with Madhu Balakrishnan's heartfelt rendition, creates an immersive experience that resonates with the audience.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Pichai Paathiram from Naan Kadavul is a soul-stirring melody that beautifully captures the essence of devotion and longing. With the enchanting voice of Madhu Balakrishnan and the masterful composition by Ilaiyaraaja, this song resonates deeply with listeners. Experience the profound emotions woven into this timeless piece that transcends mere words.
stanzaMeanings: I have come as a vessel of charity, oh my Lord, my Lord.
The body is a vessel, with bones and flesh, it is a body that is bound.
I have come as a vessel of charity, oh my Lord, my Lord.
The body is a vessel, with bones and flesh, it is a body that is bound.
I have come as a vessel of charity, oh my Lord, my Lord.
There is no mother or father, the fate of the beginning is entangled.
I do not know this present, I do not know this present, awaken the truth in the small doll's position.
I have come as a vessel of charity, oh my Lord, my Lord.
All that wealth is in your presence, I am a vessel for charity, where is it? It is just a vessel in my place, will it flow in your presence?
Once, twice, many times, you have made me take many births.
New grace, old grace, every moment you have made me suffer.
Life that is bound by material things is a burden.
Today, the heart that calls out for your grace is troubled.
With your grace, looking at me, the flower blooms, your holy hand embraces me, your grace calls me.
I have come as a vessel of charity, oh my Lord, my Lord.
The body is a vessel, with bones and flesh, it is a body that is bound.
I have come as a vessel of charity, oh my Lord, my Lord.
